session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand: 1. How `context_for_test` works - specifically what parameters it accepts and how to pass a git gateway and repo context. Look at `src/erk/core/context.py` for the `context_for_test` function. 2. How `NoRepoSentinel` works - where it's defined and how `ctx.repo_root` interacts with it. 3. How `FakeGit` works - specifically if it has a `get_current_branch` method and how to configure it with branch data. Look in `erk_shared/gateway/git/` for the fake. 4. What `RepoContext` looks like and how to create one.
Return the relevant function signatures and patterns for creating test contexts with git branch support.
session.assistant: I'll help you understand these testing patterns. Let me start by checking the documentation, then examine the relevant source files.

1→--- 2→title: context_for_test() Dual Implementations 3→read_when: 4→ - "using context_for_test() in tests" 5→ - "choosing between erk-shared and src/erk test context" 6→ - "debugging TypeError from context_for_test()" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using context_for_test() with wrong parameter name for issues" 9→ warning: "erk-shared uses github_issues= parameter, src/erk uses issues= parameter. These are NOT interchangeable — using wrong name causes TypeError." 10→--- 11→ 12→# context_for_test() Dual Implementations 13→ 14→Two separate `context_for_test()` functions exist in the codebase. They have different parameter names and serve different test scopes. 15→ 16→## erk-shared Implementation 17→ 18→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py, context_for_test --> 19→ 20→**Location:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` 21→ 22→**Key parameter:** `github_issues=` (not `issues=`) 23→ 24→Always defaults to PlannedPRBackend. Used for isolated unit tests that only need erk-shared dependencies. 25→ 26→## src/erk Implementation 27→ 28→<!-- Source: src/erk/core/context.py, context_for_test --> 29→ 30→**Location:** `src/erk/core/context.py`
... [22 lines omitted] ...
46→Using `issues=` with the erk-shared version or `github_issues=` with the src/erk version causes a `TypeError` at runtime. The parameter names are intentionally different due to the different dependency scopes.

1→--- 2→title: Test Context Composition 3→read_when: 4→ - "using build_workspace_test_context with custom fakes" 5→ - "debugging invisible metadata writes in tests" 6→ - "understanding issues_explicitly_passed flag" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating custom FakeGitHubIssues without passing to test context builder" 9→ warning: "Always pass issues=issues to build_workspace_test_context when using custom FakeGitHubIssues. Without it, plan_backend operates on a different instance and metadata writes are invisible." 10→--- 11→ 12→# Test Context Composition 13→ 14→When building test contexts with custom fakes, the composition matters — particularly for `FakeGitHubIssues` instances that are shared across multiple consumers. 15→ 16→## The Shared Instance Problem 17→ 18→`build_workspace_test_context()` creates a default `FakeGitHubIssues` if none is passed. If you create your own `FakeGitHubIssues` but don't pass it to the context builder, two separate instances exist: 19→ 20→1. Your instance (which you assert against) 21→2. The context builder's internal instance (which `plan_backend` uses) 22→ 23→Metadata writes through `plan_backend` go to instance #2, invisible to your assertions on instance #1. 24→ 25→## Correct Pattern 26→ 27→```python 28→def test_metadata_is_written(env: ErkInMemEnv) -> None: 29→ issues = FakeGitHubIssues(issues={42: IssueInfo( 30→ number=42, title="Plan", body="# Plan\n\nContent", ...
... [28 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Fake git operations for testing. 2→ 3→FakeGit is an in-memory implementation that accepts pre-configured state 4→in its constructor. 1→"""Context types for erk and erk-kits. 2→ 3→This module provides the core data types used by ErkContext: 4→- RepoContext: Repository discovery result 5→- NoRepoSentinel: Sentinel for when not in a repository 6→- GlobalConfig: Global erk configuration 7→- LoadedConfig: Repository-level configuration 8→- InteractiveAgentConfig: Configuration for interactive agent launches (Claude or Codex) 9→- AgentBackend: Type for agent backend selection 10→- PermissionMode: Generic permission mode for both Claude and Codex 11→""" 12→ 13→from __future__ import annotations 14→ 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from functools import cache 17→from pathlib import Path 18→from typing import Literal, Self 19→ 20→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId 21→ 22→# Agent backend types 23→AgentBackend = Literal["claude", "codex"] 24→ 25→# Claude CLI permission modes: 26→# - "default": Default mode with permission prompts 27→# - "acceptEdits": Accept edits without prompts (--permission-mode acceptEdits) 28→# - "plan": Plan mode for exploration and planning (--permission-mode plan) 29→# - "bypassPermissions": Bypass all permissions (--permission-mode bypassPermissions) 30→ClaudePermissionMode = Literal["default", "acceptEdits", "plan", "bypassPermissions"]

100→ 101→ 102→@dataclass(frozen=True) 103→class RepoContext: 104→ """Represents a git repo root and its managed worktrees directory. 105→ 106→ Attributes: 107→ root: The actual working tree root (where git commands run). 108→ For worktrees, this is the worktree directory. 109→ For main repos, this equals main_repo_root. 110→ repo_name: Name of the repository (derived from main_repo_root). 111→ repo_dir: Path to erk metadata directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>). 112→ worktrees_dir: Path to worktrees directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees). 113→ main_repo_root: The main repository root (for consistent metadata paths). 114→ For worktrees, this is the parent repo's root directory. 115→ For main repos, this equals root. 116→ Defaults to root for backwards compatibility. 117→ github: GitHub repository identity, if available. 118→ """ 119→ 120→ root: Path 121→ repo_name: str 122→ repo_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name> 123→ worktrees_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees 124→ pool_json_path: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/pool.json 125→ main_repo_root: Path | None = None # Defaults to root for backwards compatibility 126→ github: GitHubRepoId | None = None # None if not a GitHub repo or no remote 127→ 128→ def __post_init__(self) -> None: 129→ """Set main_repo_root to root if not provided."""
... [175 lines omitted] ...

280→ """ 281→ self._created_branches = created_branches 282→ self._deleted_branches = deleted_branches 283→ self._checked_out_branches = checked_out_branches 284→ self._detached_checkouts = detached_checkouts 285→ self._created_tracking_branches = created_tracking_branches 286→ self._updated_refs = updated_refs 287→ 288→ # ============================================================================ 289→ # Query Operations 290→ # ============================================================================ 291→ 292→ def get_current_branch(self, cwd: Path) -> str | None: 293→ """Get the currently checked-out branch.""" 294→ return self._current_branches.get(cwd) 295→ 296→ def list_local_branches(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[str]: 297→ """List all local branch names in the repository.""" 298→ return self._local_branches.get(repo_root, []) 299→ 300→ def list_remote_branches(self, repo_root: Path) -> list[str]: 301→ """List all remote branch names in the repository.""" 302→ return self._remote_branches.get(repo_root, []) 303→ 304→ def get_branch_head(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> str | None: 305→ """Get the commit SHA at the head of a branch.""" 306→ return self._branch_heads.get(branch) 307→ 308→ def get_all_branch_heads(self, repo_root: Path) -> dict[str, str]: 309→ """Get commit SHAs for all local branches."""

1→"""High-level git operations interface. 2→ 3→This module provides a clean abstraction over git subprocess calls, making the 4→codebase more testable and maintainable. 5→ 6→Architecture: 7→- Git: Abstract base class defining the interface 8→- RealGit: Production implementation using subprocess 9→- Standalone functions: Convenience wrappers delegating to module singleton 10→""" 11→ 12→from __future__ import annotations 13→ 14→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from pathlib import Path 17→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, NamedTuple 18→ 19→if TYPE_CHECKING: 20→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 21→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.abc import GitBranchOps 22→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.abc import GitCommitOps 23→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.abc import GitConfigOps 24→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.abc import GitRebaseOps 25→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 26→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.abc import GitRepoOps 27→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.abc import GitStatusOps 28→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.abc import GitTagOps 29→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 30→
... [75 lines omitted] ...

100→ 101→class Git(ABC): 102→ """Abstract interface for git operations. 103→ 104→ All implementations (real and fake) must implement this interface. 105→ This interface contains ONLY runtime operations - no test setup methods. 106→ """ 107→ 108→ @property 109→ @abstractmethod 110→ def worktree(self) -> Worktree: 111→ """Access worktree operations subgateway.""" 112→ ... 113→ 114→ @property 115→ @abstractmethod 116→ def branch(self) -> GitBranchOps: 117→ """Access branch operations subgateway.""" 118→ ... 119→ 120→ @property 121→ @abstractmethod 122→ def remote(self) -> GitRemoteOps: 123→ """Access remote operations subgateway.""" 124→ ... 125→ 126→ @property 127→ @abstractmethod 128→ def commit(self) -> GitCommitOps: 129→ """Access commit operations subgateway."""
... [43 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Tests for require_context helper function.""" 2→ 3→from typing import Any 4→ 5→import click 6→import pytest 7→ 8→from erk_shared.context.helpers import require_context 9→from erk_shared.context.testing import context_for_test 10→ 11→ 12→def test_require_context_returns_context_when_valid() -> None: 13→ """Test require_context returns ErkContext when ctx.obj is valid.""" 14→ # Arrange: Create valid ErkContext and Click context 15→ erk_ctx = context_for_test() 16→ click_ctx = click.Context(click.Command("test")) 17→ click_ctx.obj = erk_ctx 18→ 19→ # Act 20→ result = require_context(click_ctx) 21→ 22→ # Assert 23→ assert result is erk_ctx 24→ 25→ 26→def test_require_context_exits_when_ctx_obj_is_none(capsys: Any) -> None: 27→ """Test require_context exits with code 1 when ctx.obj is None.""" 28→ # Arrange: Create Click context with obj = None 29→ click_ctx = click.Context(click.Command("test")) 30→ click_ctx.obj = None
... [33 lines omitted] ...
38→ # Verify error message was printed to stderr 40→ assert "Error: Context not initialized" in captured.err 55→ # Verify error message was printed to stderr 57→ assert "Error: Context must be ErkContext" in captured.err

from erk_shared.context.testing import context_for_test
def test_require_context_returns_context_when_valid() -> None: """Test require_context returns ErkContext when ctx.obj is valid.""" # Arrange: Create valid ErkContext and Click context erk_ctx = context_for_test() click_ctx = click.Context(click.Command("test")) click_ctx.obj = erk_ctx
# Act result = require_context(click_ctx)
# Assert assert result is erk_ctx
def test_require_context_exits_when_ctx_obj_is_none(capsys: Any) -> None: """Test require_context exits with code 1 when ctx.obj is None.""" # Arrange: Create Click context with obj = None click_ctx = click.Context(click.Command("test")) click_ctx.obj = None
# Act & Assert with pytest.raises(SystemExit) as exc_info: require_context(click_ctx)

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary:
## Summary: Testing Context Setup with Git Branch Support
I've thoroughly explored the codebase and found comprehensive patterns for creating test contexts with git gateway and repository context. Here are the key findings:
### 1. context_for_test() - Two Implementations
**Important:** There are TWO separate `context_for_test()` functions with different parameter names:
#### src/erk Implementation **Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/core/context.py` (lines 202-448)
**Key parameter:** `issues=` (not `github_issues=`)
**Signature:** ```python def context_for_test( *, git: Git | None = None, github: GitHub | None = None, github_admin: GitHubAdmin | None = None, issues: GitHubIssues | None = None, # <-- KEY: issues= not github_issues= plan_store: PlanBackend | None = None, graphite: Graphite | None = None, console: Console | None = None, shell: Shell | None = None, codespace: Codespace | None = None, agent_launcher: AgentLauncher | None = None, agent_docs: AgentDocs | None = None, completion: Completion | None = None, time: Time | None = None, erk_installation: ErkInstallation | None = None, script_writer: ScriptWriter | None = None, plan_list_service: PlanListService | None = None, objective_list_service: ObjectiveListService | None = None, codespace_registry: CodespaceRegistry | None = None, claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation | None = None, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None = None, llm_caller: LlmCaller | None = None, cwd: Path | None = None, global_config: GlobalConfig | None = None, local_config: LoadedConfig | None = None, repo: RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel | None = None, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None, health_check_runner: HealthCheckRunner | None = None, dry_run: bool = False, debug: bool = False, ) -> ErkContext ```
#### erk-shared Implementation **Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py` (lines 43-224)
**Key parameter:** `github_issues=` (not `issues=`)
**Signature:** ```python def context_for_test( *, github_issues: GitHubIssues | None = None, # <-- KEY: github_issues= not issues= git: Git | None = None, github: GitHub | None = None, github_admin: GitHubAdmin | None = None, graphite: Graphite | None = None, claude_installation: ClaudeInstallation | None = None, agent_launcher: AgentLauncher | None = None, agent_docs: AgentDocs | None = None, prompt_executor: PromptExecutor | None = None, llm_caller: LlmCaller | None = None, codespace: Codespace | None = None, plan_store: PlanBackend | None = None, local_config: LoadedConfig | None = None, debug: bool = False, repo_root: Path | None = None, cwd: Path | None = None, repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None, ) -> ErkContext ```
**Decision tree:** Use src/erk version for CLI tests. Use erk-shared version only for isolated unit tests of erk-shared components.
### 2. NoRepoSentinel
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py` (lines 135-144)
**Definition:** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class NoRepoSentinel: """Sentinel value indicating execution outside a git repository.
Used when commands run outside git repositories (e.g., before init, in non-git directories). Commands that require repo context can check for this sentinel and fail fast. """ message: str = "Not inside a git repository" ```
**Usage in context_for_test():** ```python # Default when repo is not provided repo = NoRepoSentinel()
# Or explicitly: ctx = context_for_test(repo=NoRepoSentinel())
# Check in code: if isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel): # Not in a repository pass else: # ctx.repo is RepoContext repo_root = ctx.repo.root ```
### 3. RepoContext
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py` (lines 102-132)
**Definition:** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class RepoContext: """Represents a git repo root and its managed worktrees directory.
Attributes: root: The actual working tree root (where git commands run). For worktrees, this is the worktree directory. For main repos, this equals main_repo_root. repo_name: Name of the repository (derived from main_repo_root). repo_dir: Path to erk metadata directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>). worktrees_dir: Path to worktrees directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees). main_repo_root: The main repository root (for consistent metadata paths). For worktrees, this is the parent repo's root directory. For main repos, this equals root. Defaults to root for backwards compatibility. github: GitHub repository identity, if available. """ root: Path repo_name: str repo_dir: Path worktrees_dir: Path pool_json_path: Path main_repo_root: Path | None = None # Auto-set to root if None github: GitHubRepoId | None = None ```
**Creation pattern:** ```python repo = RepoContext( root=env.cwd, repo_name=env.cwd.name, repo_dir=repo_dir, worktrees_dir=repo_dir / "worktrees", pool_json_path=repo_dir / "pool.json", ) ```
### 4. FakeGit - Branch Support
**Location:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/fake.py` (lines 64-350+)
**Key Parameters for Branch Configuration:**
```python FakeGit( # Branch tracking at specific paths current_branches: dict[Path, str | None] | None = None,
# Local and remote branches for a repo local_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None, remote_branches: dict[Path, list[str]] | None = None,
# Default/trunk branches default_branches: dict[Path, str] | None = None, trunk_branches: dict[Path, str] | None = None,
# Branch head commit SHAs branch_heads: dict[str, str] | None = None,
# Worktree information worktrees: dict[Path, list[WorktreeInfo]] | None = None,
# ... and many other configuration parameters ) ```
**How to access current branch in code:** ```python # Through the branch subgateway current_branch = git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd)
# Or check mutation tracking assert git.checked_out_branches # List of (cwd, branch) tuples ```
**Complete Example from Tests:** ```python from pathlib import Path from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit from erk.core.context import context_for_test from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext
# Setup paths repo_root = Path("/test/repo") worktree_path = Path("/test/worktrees/wt-1") cwd = repo_root
# Configure FakeGit with branch state git_ops = FakeGit( # Current branch at specific paths current_branches={ cwd: "main", worktree_path: "feature-auth" }, # Available local branches local_branches={ cwd: ["main", "feature-auth", "bugfix-123"] }, # Remote branches (with origin/ prefix) remote_branches={ cwd: ["origin/main", "origin/remote-feature"] }, # Worktree information worktrees={ repo_root: [ WorktreeInfo(path=repo_root, branch="main", is_root=True), WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-auth", is_root=False) ] }, # Git directories git_common_dirs={cwd: repo_root / ".git"}, # Default and trunk branches default_branches={cwd: "main"}, trunk_branches={cwd: "main"}, )
# Create RepoContext repo = RepoContext( root=cwd, repo_name="my-repo", repo_dir=Path("/fake/erk/repos/my-repo"), worktrees_dir=Path("/fake/erk/repos/my-repo/worktrees"), pool_json_path=Path("/fake/erk/repos/my-repo/pool.json"), )
# Create test context (src/erk version) ctx = context_for_test( git=git_ops, repo=repo, )
# Access branch information current = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd) # Returns "main" branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(cwd) # Returns ["main", "feature-auth", "bugfix-123"] ```
### 5. Git Interface Structure
The Git gateway is composed of sub-gateways accessible via properties:
```python # Through ctx.git or git object git.branch # GitBranchOps - branch operations git.remote # GitRemoteOps - remote operations git.commit # GitCommitOps - commit operations git.status # GitStatusOps - status operations git.rebase # GitRebaseOps - rebase operations git.tag # GitTagOps - tag operations git.repo # GitRepoOps - repository location operations git.analysis # GitAnalysisOps - branch analysis git.config # GitConfigOps - configuration git.worktree # Worktree - worktree operations ```
**Getting current branch (most common use):** ```python branch = git.branch.get_current_branch(cwd: Path) -> str | None ```
### 6. Key Tripwires
From the documentation:
- **Parameter name difference:** Using `issues=` with erk-shared version or `github_issues=` with src/erk version causes TypeError - **Shared instance problem:** When creating custom FakeGitHubIssues, must pass it to context builder so plan_backend uses same instance - **context_for_test imports:** - CLI tests: `from erk.core.context import context_for_test` - erk-shared unit tests: `from erk_shared.context.testing import context_for_test`
### 7. File Locations Summary
| Component | Location | |-----------|----------| | `context_for_test()` src/erk | `/src/erk/core/context.py:202` | | `context_for_test()` erk-shared | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/testing.py:43` | | `RepoContext` | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py:102` | | `NoRepoSentinel` | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py:135` | | `FakeGit` | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/fake.py:64` | | `Git` ABC | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/abc.py:101` | | `GitBranchOps` fake | `/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/fake.py:292` |
### 8. Testing Documentation References
- `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/testing/context-builder-signatures.md` - Dual implementations docs - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/test-context-composition.md` - Shared instance patterns - `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/unit/cli/commands/wt/test_create_from_cmd.py` - Real-world examples
